blocks.body: Extended passive voice grammar is the intermediate level of learning passive structures. Here you will learn how the passive is formed in Continuous and Perfect tenses, as well as with modal verbs.

{"level":"B1","intro":"In this topic you will learn the passive voice in different tenses: Continuous, Perfect, Future, and with modal verbs.","rules":[{"label":"Continuous","color":"emerald","text":"Continuous tenses: be + being + V3:","example":"The house is being painted."},{"label":"Perfect","color":"blue","text":"Perfect tenses: have/had + been + V3:","example":"The letter has been sent."},{"label":"Modals","color":"rose","text":"With modal verbs: modal + be + V3:","example":"This must be done today."}]}

{"title":"1. Present Continuous Passive","sections":[{"label":"Structure","color":"emerald","description":"Formula: am/is/are + being + Past Participle (V3)","examples":[{"en":"The house is being painted right now.","ua":"The house is being painted right now."},{"en":"The documents are being prepared.","ua":"The documents are being prepared."},{"en":"I am being interviewed at the moment.","ua":"I am being interviewed at the moment."}]},{"label":"Use","color":"sky","description":"The action is happening now, in progress.","examples":[{"en":"The road is being repaired this week.","ua":"The road is being repaired this week."},{"en":"New software is being installed.","ua":"New software is being installed."}]}]}

{"title":"2. Past Continuous Passive","sections":[{"label":"Structure","color":"emerald","description":"Formula: was/were + being + Past Participle (V3)","examples":[{"en":"The car was being repaired when I arrived.","ua":"The car was being repaired when I arrived."},{"en":"The rooms were being cleaned all morning.","ua":"The rooms were being cleaned all morning."}]},{"label":"Use","color":"sky","description":"The action was in progress in the past at a certain moment.","examples":[{"en":"While I was waiting, my application was being processed.","ua":"While I was waiting, my application was being processed."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Present Perfect Passive","sections":[{"label":"Structure","color":"emerald","description":"Formula: has/have + been + Past Participle (V3)","examples":[{"en":"The project has been completed.","ua":"The project has been completed."},{"en":"All tickets have been sold.","ua":"All tickets have been sold."},{"en":"I have been promoted!","ua":"I have been promoted!"}]},{"label":"Use","color":"sky","description":"The action has been completed, and the result is important now.","examples":[{"en":"The report has just been submitted.","ua":"The report has just been submitted."},{"en":"The files have already been deleted.","ua":"The files have already been deleted."}]}]}

{"title":"4. Past Perfect Passive","sections":[{"label":"Structure","color":"emerald","description":"Formula: had + been + Past Participle (V3)","examples":[{"en":"The work had been finished before the deadline.","ua":"The work had been finished before the deadline."},{"en":"The tickets had been sold out before we arrived.","ua":"The tickets had been sold out before we arrived."}]},{"label":"Use","color":"sky","description":"The action was completed before another past action.","examples":[{"en":"By the time I got there, the problem had been solved.","ua":"By the time I got there, the problem had been solved."}]}]}

{"title":"5. Future Simple Passive","sections":[{"label":"Structure","color":"emerald","description":"Formula: will + be + Past Participle (V3)","examples":[{"en":"The results will be announced tomorrow.","ua":"The results will be announced tomorrow."},{"en":"The meeting will be held next Monday.","ua":"The meeting will be held next Monday."},{"en":"You will be contacted soon.","ua":"You will be contacted soon."}]},{"label":"Negatives and questions","color":"sky","description":"Negative: will not (won't) + be + V3. Question: Will + S + be + V3?","examples":[{"en":"The project will not be finished on time.","ua":"The project will not be finished on time."},{"en":"Will the report be ready by Friday?","ua":"Will the report be ready by Friday?"}]}]}

{"title":"6. Passive with modal verbs","sections":[{"label":"Structure","color":"emerald","description":"Formula: modal + be + Past Participle (V3)","examples":[{"en":"This task must be done today.","ua":"This task must be done today."},{"en":"The rules should be followed.","ua":"The rules should be followed."},{"en":"The document can be downloaded.","ua":"The document can be downloaded."}]},{"label":"Different modals","color":"sky","description":"It works with all modal verbs: can, could, may, might, must, should, have to.","examples":[{"en":"The work has to be finished.","ua":"The work has to be finished."},{"en":"Mistakes might be made.","ua":"Mistakes might be made."},{"en":"This could be improved.","ua":"This could be improved."}]}]}

{"title":"7. Summary table of passive tenses","intro":"Passive structures in different tenses:","rows":[{"en":"Present Simple","ua":"am/is/are + V3","note":"The letter is written."},{"en":"Past Simple","ua":"was/were + V3","note":"The letter was written."},{"en":"Present Continuous","ua":"am/is/are + being + V3","note":"The letter is being written."},{"en":"Past Continuous","ua":"was/were + being + V3","note":"The letter was being written."},{"en":"Present Perfect","ua":"has/have + been + V3","note":"The letter has been written."},{"en":"Past Perfect","ua":"had + been + V3","note":"The letter had been written."},{"en":"Future Simple","ua":"will + be + V3","note":"The letter will be written."},{"en":"Modals","ua":"modal + be + V3","note":"The letter must be written."}]}

{"title":"8. Quick summary","items":["Continuous Passive: be + being + V3 — an action in progress.","Perfect Passive: have/had + been + V3 — a completed action.","Future Passive: will + be + V3 — a future action.","Modal Passive: modal + be + V3 — with modal verbs.","The element be changes according to the tense, while V3 stays the same.","Future Perfect Passive: will have been + V3 (rarely used)."]}
